issues.description: Fleet summary `total_cost` is hardcoded to `0.0` — the `compute_fleet_summary` function never computes actual token costs because it doesn't fetch round data. The comment says 'computed properly in the dashboard_state endpoint with round data' but neither `grid_page` nor `dashboard_state` overrides the `total_cost` field before using it. FR-9a requires '$X.XX — total token cost' in the fleet summary, which will always render as '$0.00'.
issues.suggestion: Pass round data into `compute_fleet_summary` (or fetch it inside the function) so that `total_cost` is computed from actual `token_usage` data. Alternatively, compute it at the call site and patch the returned `FleetSummary`.

issues.description: Fleet summary `top_spender` uses loop count × $0.25 as a cost proxy instead of actual token cost. FR-9a requires 'top: <engineer> ($X.XX)' showing the highest-spender's real cost. This will display fabricated dollar amounts.
issues.suggestion: Compute actual per-engineer cost by summing `compute_round_metrics` for each engineer's loops within the 7-day window.

issues.description: N+1 query problem in `dashboard_state`: iterates all loops and calls `get_rounds()` per loop, plus `resolve_current_stage()` which may call `get_rounds()` again for Hardening state. For 50 active loops this is 50-100 DB queries. NFR-2 requires <200ms for 50 active loops. The whole point of FR-8b's `/dashboard/state` endpoint was to avoid N+1 patterns.
issues.suggestion: Add a bulk `get_rounds_for_loops(loop_ids: &[Uuid])` method to the state store, or cache rounds fetched for `compute_round_metrics` so `resolve_current_stage` doesn't re-fetch.

issues.description: In `compute_stats`, loops are iterated TWICE with `get_rounds()` called per loop each time (once for per-engineer aggregation, once for per-spec aggregation). For N loops in the window this is 2N round queries. Combined with the 60s cache, this is expensive on cache miss.
issues.suggestion: Fetch rounds once per loop, compute both per-engineer and per-spec aggregates in a single pass.

issues.description: CSS and JS assets are included twice in the binary: once in `render.rs` (inlined into every HTML page via <style>/<script> tags) and once in `handlers.rs` (served as static files). Each page carries ~30KB of inline assets that are never cached by the browser. This likely violates NFR-2's '<100 KB uncompressed, <30 KB gzipped' page weight target and wastes bandwidth on every navigation.
issues.suggestion: Remove inline CSS/JS from `render.rs`. Use `<link rel="stylesheet" href="/dashboard/static/dashboard.css">` and `<script src="/dashboard/static/dashboard.js"></script>` in the layout template instead, leveraging the static file handlers that already exist with `max-age=86400` caching.

issues.description: In `grid_page`, loops are fetched twice from the store with identical parameters — once for building cards and once for computing filter counts (`all_loops`). This doubles DB load unnecessarily.
issues.suggestion: Compute counts from the original `loops` vec before applying the state filter, eliminating the second query.

issues.description: FR-9b requires trend indicators comparing current vs prior 7-day window (e.g., '82% ↑8% converged'). The implementation has no prior-period comparison logic — trend indicators are completely missing.
issues.suggestion: Compute prior-window aggregates (days -14 to -7) alongside the current window and render delta arrows when prior data exists.

issues.description: FR-12b requires per-engineer filter chips on the feed page. The implementation only has 'All events' / 'Converged only' / 'Failed only' chips, missing individual engineer filtering.
issues.suggestion: Add per-engineer chips to the feed page, similar to the grid page's engineer chip bar.

issues.description: The raw API key is interpolated into the Set-Cookie header via `format!()` without any encoding or validation of characters. If the API key contains semicolons, newlines, or other special characters, this enables cookie attribute injection or HTTP header injection.
issues.suggestion: URL-encode the API key value before placing it in the cookie, or validate that the key contains only safe ASCII characters before setting the cookie.

issues.description: Multiple tests use `unsafe { std::env::set_var() }` which is unsound in multi-threaded contexts (and formally UB since Rust 1.66+ when multiple threads exist). Tokio tests run on a multi-threaded runtime by default. Different tests setting different values for NAUTILOOP_API_KEY can race with each other.
issues.suggestion: Use `#[tokio::test(flavor = "current_thread")]` for these tests, or refactor auth to accept the expected key as a parameter/config instead of reading from env var directly in the hot path.

issues.description: The stats cache (`STATS_CACHE`) is a process-global `OnceLock<RwLock<StatsCache>>` that is shared across all tests. Tests manually clear it but this is fragile — concurrent test execution can still interfere. The cache also doesn't distinguish by `AppState`, so cached data from one test leaks into another.
issues.suggestion: Move the cache into `AppState` so each test gets its own cache instance.

issues.description: Auth middleware reads `NAUTILOOP_API_KEY` from environment on every request. If the env var is unset, it returns 500 Internal Server Error. This is the correct behavior, but in the existing API auth (`auth.rs`), the key may be loaded differently (e.g., from config). The dashboard auth is inconsistent with whatever the main API auth does.
issues.suggestion: Load the expected API key from `AppState.config` instead of directly from the environment, matching how the main API auth works.

issues.description: The `maud` crate with `features = ["axum"]` pulls in `axum-core 0.4.5` alongside the app's `axum-core 0.5.6`. While the code works around this by calling `.into_string()` instead of relying on maud's `IntoResponse` impl, the axum feature flag is unnecessary overhead that introduces a duplicate semver-incompatible dependency.
issues.suggestion: Remove `features = ["axum"]` from the maud dependency in `Cargo.toml` since the axum integration trait isn't used — all rendering goes through `.into_string()` wrapped in `Html()`.

issues.description: FR-9c says tapping any individual field in the fleet summary should navigate to `/dashboard/stats` with that field pre-focused. The implementation wraps the entire summary in a single `<a href="/dashboard/stats">` link without field-level targeting.
issues.suggestion: Wrap each metric field in its own link with a query param or anchor (e.g., `/dashboard/stats?focus=cost`).

issues.description: FR-12b specifies filter selection should persist in localStorage across visits. The implementation uses URL query params and `<a>` links instead. Technically a spec deviation, though URL params are arguably better UX.
issues.suggestion: Add JS to save filter selection to localStorage and restore it on page load when no URL param is present.

issues.description: FR-2b says the login form should validate the key 'by making an internal `/status` call'. The implementation validates directly against the env var. Functionally equivalent and arguably better, but doesn't exercise the same code path a real API consumer would.
issues.suggestion: Consider this acceptable as-is; the direct check is simpler and avoids a self-referential HTTP call.
